test_title: NPAW-1.6 — replay after ended opens new Youbora session
error_message: Timeout waiting for 'contentFirstPlay' event after player.play() on ended video. n1 not > n0 on retry.
root_cause: The test waits for 'contentFirstPlay' after calling player.play() on an ended video, but Lightning Player v1.0.67 does not re-emit 'contentFirstPlay' on replay — the event fires only once per content load. After ended, calling play() transitions the player to 'playing' but the harness never adds 'contentFirstPlay' to __qa.events again, so waitForEvent() times out.
suspected_cause: Test assumes 'contentFirstPlay' is emitted again on replay (like a new load), but the player treats replay as a continuation of the same session. The correct signal for 'playback resumed after ended' is 'playing'. The test should wait for 'playing' after the play() call, then check beacon count increase. Additionally, the test clears __qa.events and polls n1 → n0 comparison but the Youbora tracker's fireStart guard (if _started) would also block new beacons if _started was not reset by onEnded — those are two separate issues: the test defect (wrong wait event) and a potential player bug (tracker _started reset on ended). The test defect must be fixed first to validate whether the tracker actually fires new beacons on replay.
